translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the second year of Darius the king, in the sixth month, on the first day of the month, came the word of Jehovah by the prophet Haggai unto Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and to Joshua the son of Jehozadak, the high priest, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus speaketh Jehovah of hosts, saying, This people say, The time is not come, the time that Jehovah's house should be built.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came by Haggai the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is it time for you that ye should dwell in your wainscoted houses, while this house lieth waste?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Consider your ways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have sown much, and bring in little; ye eat, but are not satisfied; ye drink, but are not filled with drink; ye clothe yourselves, but there is none warm; and he that earneth wages earneth wages for a bag with holes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Consider your ways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go up to the mountain and bring wood, and build the house, and I will take pleasure in it, and I will be glorified, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye looked for much, and behold it was little; and when ye brought it home, I blew upon it. Wherefore? saith Jehovah of hosts. Because of my house that lieth waste, whilst ye run every man to his own house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore over you the heavens withhold their dew, and the earth withholdeth its fruit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I called for a drought upon the land, and upon the mountains, and upon the corn, and upon the new wine, and upon the oil, and upon that which the ground bringeth forth, and upon man, and upon cattle, and upon all the labour of the hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Joshua the son of Jehozadak, the high priest, and all the remnant of the people, hearkened to the voice of Jehovah theirGod, and the words of Haggai the prophet, according as Jehovah theirGod had sent him, and the people feared before Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then spoke Haggai, Jehovah's messenger, in Jehovah's message unto the people, saying, I am with you, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah stirred up the spirit of Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and the spirit of Joshua the son of Jehozadak, the high priest, and the spirit of all the remnant of the people; and they came and worked at the house of Jehovah of hosts, theirGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in the four and twentieth day of the sixth month, in the second year of Darius the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the seventh [month], on the one and twentieth [day] of the month, came the word of Jehovah by the prophet Haggai, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak now to Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and to Joshua the son of Jehozadak, the high priest, and to the remnant of the people, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is left among you that saw this house in its former glory? and how do ye see it now? Is it not as nothing in your eyes?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now be strong, Zerubbabel, saith Jehovah; and be strong, Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest; and be strong, all ye people of the land, saith Jehovah, and work: for I am with you, saith Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word that I covenanted with you when ye came out of Egypt, and my Spirit, remain among you: fear ye not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Yet once, it is a little while, and I will shake the heavens, and the earth, and the sea, and the dry [land];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will shake all nations, and the desire of all nations shall come; and I will fill this house with glory, saith Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The silver is mine, and the gold is mine, saith Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The latter glory of this house shall be greater than the former, saith Jehovah of hosts; and in this place will I give peace, saith Jehovah of hosts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the four and twentieth [day] of the ninth [month], in the second year of Darius, came the word of Jehovah by Haggai the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Ask now the priests [concerning] the law, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If one bear holy flesh in the skirt of his garment, and with his skirt do touch bread, or pottage, or wine, or oil, or any food — shall it become holy? And the priests answered and said, No.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Haggai said, If one that is unclean by a dead body touch any of these, is it become unclean? And the priests answered and said, It shall be unclean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then answered Haggai and said, So is this people, and so is this nation before me, saith Jehovah, and so is every work of their hands; and that which they offer there is unclean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, I pray you, consider from this day and onward, from before a stone was laid upon a stone in the temple of Jehovah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — before those [days] were, when one came to a heap of twenty [measures], there were but ten; when one came to the vat to draw out fifty press-measures, there were but twenty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I smote you with blasting and with mildew and with hail in all the work of your hands; and ye [turned] not to me, saith Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Consider, I pray you, from this day and onward, from the four and twentieth day of the ninth [month], from the day that the foundation of Jehovah's temple was laid, consider [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is the seed yet in the barn? yea, the vine, and the fig-tree, and the pomegranate, and the olive-tree have not brought forth: from this day will I bless [you].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came the second time unto Haggai on the four and twentieth [day] of the month, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak to Zerubbabel, governor of Judah, saying, I will shake the heavens and the earth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will overthrow the throne of kingdoms, and I will destroy the strength of the kingdoms of the nations; and I will overthrow the chariots, and those that ride therein; and the horses and their riders shall come down, every one by the sword of his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In that day, saith Jehovah of hosts, will I take thee, Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, my servant, saith Jehovah, and will make thee as a signet; for I have chosen thee, saith Jehovah of hosts.
